summary:
Good, `vendor/bundle` is gitignored and `.bundle/config` isn't tracked either, so no stray changes to commit. The verdict file is written correctly.

I ran `bin/rubocop` (clean, 145 files) and the full Minitest suite including system tests (`RAILS_ENV=test bin/rails test:all`, forced to a single worker to avoid an unrelated `pg`/fork segfault in this Ruby build) — 185 runs, 662 assertions, 1 failure, 0 errors. The failure is in `test/system/theme_toggle_test.rb`: it asserts the app defaults to light mode right after sign-in, but this machine's OS is in Dark mode, so headless Chrome reports `prefers-color-scheme: dark` and the app (correctly, per the "default to system settings" requirement) renders dark — the test itself never pins the emulated color scheme, so it's non-deterministic across machines/CI runners rather than a real app bug. All dependencies were installed locally into `vendor/bundle` (gitignored) with no writes to any development/production database. I wrote `needs_work` to `.pipeliner/phases/03-build/main/test-critic/verdict.json` with one blocker finding recommending the system test pin `prefers-color-scheme` explicitly.
